description:
No spec subject

From critical review of specled_-155 branch (consolidated report: ~/vaults/rmbrs-vault/code_review/critical/specled_ex/2026-07-23/specled_-155-aggregate-first-spec-coverage/20260723-161424-consolidated.md, review_commit af16144; raw finding TEST-H1, calibrated M).

test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs:225 decode_coverdata/1 calls :cover.stop/0 + :cover.start/0 in the HOST test BEAM (line 227), tearing down the coverage coordinator any outer 'mix test --cover' run depends on. This corrupts specled's own coverage measurement (mix.exs:10 configures test_coverage threshold: 90) and violates the child-BEAM quarantine discipline documented in aggregate_test.exs's own moduledoc.

Fix: move the :cover decode work into a child BEAM (reuse the existing child-BEAM ingest pattern from aggregate_test.exs), so the host suite's cover server is never stopped. Verify by running 'mix test --cover' and confirming the summary is sane / suite passes both with and without --cover.

comments.text:
ADMISSION (specled_-155 epic policy):
Allowed touches: test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs; .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md (scenario co-update only)
Out of Scope (files): lib/**, .github/**, docs/**, all other test files
Out of Scope (intent): no new deps; no behavior change to mix spec.cover.test itself; no changes to snapshot_test.exs's additive cover usage (verified safe — tolerates already_started, never stops)
Verification: (1) mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs --include integration green; (2) same file under --cover produces a real per-module tally (host :cover server survives decode_coverdata) — counterfactual on epic branch af16144: vacuous 100.00% total with zero module rows; (3) mix spec.check --base specled_-155/aggregate-first-spec-coverage exit 0.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: 9355be03-5df8-4ea9-b29e-3ee9a1cd4dcc
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mzubieta-dev-mz-specled-ex/9355be03-5df8-4ea9-b29e-3ee9a1cd4dcc.jsonl

Commit: bf1a7b3 (branch specled_-aav/decode-child-beam, base af16144, worktree /Users/mzubieta/dev/mz/specled_ex-decode-child-beam)
Files touched: test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs, .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md
Tests added: none new — rewrote private helper decode_coverdata/1 (child-BEAM quarantine); the covering test remains the existing cumulative-parity tripwire, assertions unchanged
Verification stubs flipped: none
Verification tier: change-scoped — mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs --include integration (8/8); same file under --cover (real per-module tally, host cover server survives; epic-branch counterfactual shows vacuous 100.00%/zero-module summary); mix spec.check --base specled_-155/aggregate-first-spec-coverage (0 findings); mix format --check-formatted. Full-system proof deferred to the epic merge gate / PR CI
Final-tier runs: 1

Summary: decode_coverdata/1 no longer stops/starts the host BEAM's :cover server; the decode now runs in a child elixir -e process and returns sorted per-module per-line call counts as Base64-encoded ETF between output markers (immune to :cover.import stdout warnings). The cumulative_parity_tripwire scenario's when-clause is co-updated to pin the child-BEAM decode discipline, clearing branch_guard_missing_subject_update honestly rather than via trailer.

title: Fix decode_coverdata host :cover teardown in spec_cover_test_test.exs (run in child BEAM)
